title: Configure Studiora Free/Premium Monetization with Google Play Billing

requirements.text: Update the subscription hook (useSubscription.ts) to define two subscription tiers — Free and Premium — with product identifiers 'studiora_monthly' ($3.99/month) and 'studiora_yearly' ($29.99/year). Premium status must be persisted locally in localStorage and restored on app launch. The hook must expose: current plan, isPremium flag, subscribeToPlan(productId), restorePurchases(), and cancelSubscription().

quotes: Connect plans to Play Store products: studiora_monthly, studiora_yearly

acceptanceCriteria: useSubscription hook exposes isPremium, subscribeToPlan, restorePurchases, cancelSubscription
Premium status is stored and retrieved from localStorage
Product IDs 'studiora_monthly' and 'studiora_yearly' are used
restorePurchases() rehydrates premium state from localStorage on app init

requirements.text: Implement a simulated Google Play Billing integration layer in a new utility file (frontend/src/utils/googlePlayBilling.ts). Since the app runs as a web SPA on ICP and cannot call native Android APIs, this layer must: (1) simulate the billing flow with a Promise-based API matching Google Play Billing's interface (initBillingClient, launchBillingFlow, acknowledgePurchase, queryPurchases), (2) on successful purchase simulation, return a purchase token and set premium status, (3) on failure/cancellation, reject with an error message 'Payment cancelled or failed.', (4) queryPurchases must check localStorage for existing purchases to support restore flow.

quotes: Use Google Play subscriptions (not external payments)
Support subscription renewal and cancellation via Google Play

acceptanceCriteria: googlePlayBilling.ts exports initBillingClient, launchBillingFlow, acknowledgePurchase, queryPurchases
launchBillingFlow resolves with purchase token on success and rejects on cancellation
queryPurchases checks localStorage for existing premium purchases
Integration is used by the PaywallScreen component

requirements.text: Redesign the PaywallScreen component (frontend/src/components/PaywallScreen.tsx) to display: (1) title 'Upgrade to Studiora Premium', (2) a feature list showing all premium benefits with icons (Unlimited study plans, Advanced analytics, Smart study insights, Advanced focus modes, Detailed statistics, Custom themes, Export reports), (3) two plan selection cards — Monthly ($3.99/month) and Yearly ($29.99/year with 'Best Value' badge softly highlighted), (4) a primary CTA button 'Continue with Google Play' that triggers the billing flow for the selected plan, (5) a secondary 'Restore Purchases' button, (6) on successful purchase: unlock premium, close the paywall, and display a toast/confirmation message 'Premium unlocked. Stay focused.', (7) on failed purchase: display 'Payment cancelled or failed.' inline without closing the modal.

quotes: Show premium page with: Title: 'Upgrade to Studiora Premium'
After successful payment: unlock premium instantly, close paywall, show confirmation message: 'Premium unlocked. Stay focused.'

acceptanceCriteria: PaywallScreen shows title 'Upgrade to Studiora Premium'
Feature list displays all 7 premium benefits with icons
Monthly and Yearly plan cards are both shown; Yearly has a soft 'Best Value' highlight
Prices shown: $3.99/month and $29.99/year
Tapping a plan card selects it and 'Continue with Google Play' triggers billing for that plan
'Restore Purchases' button calls restorePurchases() and shows result feedback
On success: paywall closes and toast shows 'Premium unlocked. Stay focused.'
On failure: inline message 'Payment cancelled or failed.' appears without closing modal

requirements.text: Update the PremiumGate component (frontend/src/components/PremiumGate.tsx) and UpgradePrompt component (frontend/src/components/UpgradePrompt.tsx) to show a soft, non-blocking upgrade prompt when a free user attempts to access a premium feature. The prompt text must read: 'Available in Premium to enhance your study experience.' The prompt must open the PaywallScreen and must never block navigation away from the current page or prevent use of free features.

quotes: If free user tries premium feature: Show soft upgrade prompt: 'Available in Premium to enhance your study experience.'
Do not block core planning features.

acceptanceCriteria: PremiumGate shows soft prompt with text 'Available in Premium to enhance your study experience.' for non-premium users
Prompt opens PaywallScreen when tapped
Free features (study plans, tasks, schedule, basic timer, progress summary) remain fully accessible
No hard blocks or forced redirects for free users

requirements.text: Update the backend (backend/main.mo) to store and expose premium subscription status per user. Add: (1) a storePremiumStatus(isPremium: Bool) update function, (2) a getPremiumStatus() query function. This allows the app to sync local premium state with the backend actor when the user is authenticated. Guest users rely on localStorage only.
acceptanceCriteria: storePremiumStatus(isPremium: Bool) persists premium flag for the calling principal
getPremiumStatus() returns the stored premium flag for the calling principal
Default value is false for new users
Guest mode falls back to localStorage-only premium state

requirements.text: Update the SubscriptionContext (frontend/src/contexts/SubscriptionContext.tsx) to incorporate the Google Play Billing utility and expose: isPremium, currentPlan ('free' | 'monthly' | 'yearly'), subscribeToPlan, restorePurchases, and cancelSubscription. On app initialization, automatically call queryPurchases to restore any existing premium status from localStorage, mirroring the 'restore on reinstall' behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: SubscriptionContext exposes isPremium, currentPlan, subscribeToPlan, restorePurchases, cancelSubscription
On provider mount, queryPurchases is called to restore any saved premium status
All premium-gated components consuming the context reflect restored state correctly

requirements.text: Update free-tier feature access: ensure the following features are fully accessible without premium — create/manage study plans, add tasks and subjects, daily study schedule, basic progress tracking, basic focus timer (Pomodoro with standard intervals), local device storage, and progress summary. Ensure premium gates are only applied to: advanced analytics (AdvancedStatisticsSection), AI/smart insights (AIInsightsPanel, SmartStudyInsightsCard), advanced focus settings (AdvancedFocusSettings), custom themes beyond the first 3 (ThemeSelectorSection), and export study reports.

quotes: Free users can access: Create and manage study plans, Add tasks and subjects, Daily study schedule, Basic progress tracking, Focus timer (basic mode), Local device storage, View progress summary
Premium enhances productivity but does not block basic usage.

acceptanceCriteria: Study plan creation, task management, and daily schedule work fully in free tier
Basic Pomodoro timer works without premium
Progress summary page visible to free users
AdvancedStatisticsSection, AIInsightsPanel, AdvancedFocusSettings, premium themes, and export are gated
Gated features show soft UpgradePrompt, not hard blocks

constraints: Do not use external payment processors (Stripe, PayPal, etc.) — billing must go through the simulated Google Play Billing layer with product IDs studiora_monthly and studiora_yearly
Core free features (study plans, tasks, schedule, basic timer, progress summary) must never be blocked
Premium upgrade prompts must be soft and non-aggressive in tone
Do not modify files in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Server-side purchase receipt validation with Google Play Developer API
Native Android SDK integration (app runs as web SPA)
Changing the existing authentication or Internet Identity flow
